skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
x² − 26x + c = 0

In the given equation, c is a constant. The equation has no real solutions if c > n. What is the least possible value of n?
rationale: A quadratic x² + bx + c = 0 has no real solutions when its discriminant b² − 4c < 0. Here that means (−26)² − 4c < 0, or 676 < 4c, so c > 169. The least value of n is 169.
